column1: J. PROBLEMS ENCOUNTERED (before, during and after fire operations) / (chek all applicable)

column3: Inaccurate address / no landmarks especially in provinces resulting to difficulty in finding the involved
Geographically challenged (distance of fire station to community: mountainous, inter-island and the like)

Road conditions (double parking, narrow obstructed streets, rough roads, traffic lights malfunction, congested U-turn slots, merging roads, influx of pedestrians from places of assembly especially during rush hours , multiple humps, steep roads, open man holes/sewer, ambulant vendors obstruction, overhanging trees/branches, overhanging canopy of houses, dangling wires and the like)
Natural Disasters / Natural Phenomenon (Floodings / landslides, earthquakes others)
Response delays due to lack of cooperation from property security and/or owner (ex. PEZA)
Engine failure / mechanical problems while enroute
Intense heat and smoke during firefighting operation
Structural hazards (building collapse, electrical hazard, falling debris)
Equipment mulfunction (faulty hoses, overheating or any mechanical issues of fire trucks or pumps)
Lack of coordination with other emergency response teams (police, medics etc)
Breakdown in radio communication due to distance, building structures, or system failure

column2: Recommendation should solve the above stated problems encoutered.

As of this date, no complaint has been filed against this Fire Station in relation to the fire suppression operation conducted. Accordingly, this report is hereby referred to the Intelligence and Investigation Unit/Section for further verification and fire cause determination. The said unit shall update and notify the Operations Unit/Section, through an official certification, should there be any developments or findings arising from the investigation that may affect the existing data and statistics.
